MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Lapham, Esq. 925-837-9007 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Marina Pitts, Esq. 925-974-8600 of Leone & Alberts for Defendant Regents of the University of California.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court makes a disclosure and raises his concerns. Defendant Argues.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JESSEE HERMOGENO, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Judge Schulman discloses that he previously represented The Regents of the University of California. This will not affect Judge Schulman's ability to be fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendants The Regents of the University of California and Jessee Hermogeno's motions for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. (see word minutes for full details)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jul-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marina B. Pitts, Law Offices of Leone & Albert, appearing for Defendant UC Regents, Telpehone 925-974-8600;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JESSEE HERMOGENO, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Plaintiff is unopposed and granted as follows: Plaintiff is to appear for examination and testing (as described in paragraphs 5 and 6 of the supporting Declaration of Jeffrey Gould, M.D. dated July 2, 2018) on August 3, 2018, beginning at 10:00 a.m., or at such other date and time as the parties and Dr. Gould may agree. The examination is limited to 8 hours, exclusive of breaks. The examination is to be continuously audiotaped by Dr. Gould, including break times, and Dr. Gould is to provide a copy of the recording to plaintiff's counsel. For avoidance of doubt, the provisions of CCP 2032.410-2032.420, 2032.2032.510-2032.530 and 2032.610-2032.650 will apply.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
